headline: KL Mandates Stormwater Harvesting for New Developments
context: DBKL mandated stormwater harvesting for all new commercial and residential developments above 1,000 sqm effective January 2026.

cards.big: KL mandates stormwater harvesting for all new developments from Jan 2026
cards.sub: Policy aims to reduce flood runoff by 25% and supplement water supply

cards.big: Systems must capture minimum 50 litres per square metre of roof area
cards.sub: Estimated compliance cost adds RM8,000-15,000 per residential unit

cards.big: Singapore ABC Waters programme reduced urban flooding 30% since 2006
cards.sub: KL policy modelled on Singapore but lacks equivalent enforcement resources

cards.big: Only 12% of existing KL buildings have any rainwater harvesting capacity
cards.sub: Retrofit incentives of RM5,000 tax relief attracted 2,200 applications in 2 months

cards.big: Stormwater rules test whether new development standards actually reduce flood damage
Stormwater harvesting mandates shift urban water policy from drainage to capture — a structural improvement.
